message:
feat: Add production-ready training module with GaLore

Consolidate training code into src/train/ with:
- trainer.zig: Production trainer using llama.cpp Metal training
  - Checkpoint save/resume with automatic cleanup
  - Best model tracking (saves on eval loss improvement)
  - JSONL and repository data sources
  - Multi-language code extraction (Zig, Python, Rust, Go, JS, TS, C/C++)
- galore.zig: GaLore optimizer for 60-80% memory savings
  - Low-rank gradient projection/deprojection
  - AdamW updates in compressed space
  - Ready for when fine-grained gradients available
- lora_pool.zig: Adapter management with LRU eviction
- train.zig: Clean module interface with examples

Updated imports in main.zig, scheduler.zig, dashboard.zig,
hippocampus.zig, and auto_trainer.zig.

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,169 @@
+# Zell Training Module
+
+Production-ready fine-tuning for LLMs on Apple Silicon.
+
+## Features
+
+- **LoRA Training** - Metal-accelerated via llama.cpp
+- **Checkpoint Save/Resume** - Automatic best model tracking
+- **Multiple Data Sources** - JSONL files or code repositories
+- **GaLore Ready** - 60-80% memory savings when available
+- **Adapter Pool** - LRU eviction with memory budgets
+
+## Architecture
+
+```
+┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
+│              Zell Training                  │
+├─────────────────────────────────────────────┤
+│  trainer.zig - Production training loop     │
+│  - llama.cpp Metal training                 │
+│  - Checkpoint save/resume                   │
+│  - Data loading (JSONL, repo)               │
+├─────────────────────────────────────────────┤
+│  galore.zig - GaLore optimizer              │
+│  - Low-rank gradient projection             │
+│  - 60-80% memory savings                    │
+├─────────────────────────────────────────────┤
+│  lora_pool.zig - Adapter management         │
+│  - Load/unload adapters                     │
+│  - LRU eviction                             │
+│  - Memory budget tracking                   │
+├─────────────────────────────────────────────┤
+│  lora_trainer.zig - Legacy trainer          │
+│  - Backward compatible API                  │
+└─────────────────────────────────────────────┘
+```
+
+## Quick Start
+
+### Command Line
+
+```bash
+# Train from code repository
+zell lora model.gguf train my_style --from-repo /path/to/code
+
+# Train from JSONL data
+zell lora model.gguf train my_style --data training.jsonl --epochs 5
+
+# Load and use adapter
+zell lora model.gguf load my_style ~/.cache/zell/lora/my_style.lora
+zell run model.gguf "Write code in my style"
+```
+
+### Zig API
+
+```zig
+const train = @import("train/train.zig");
+
+// Simple training
+try train.trainSimple(
+    allocator,
+    "model.gguf",
+    "my_adapter.lora",
+    .{ .repo = "/path/to/code" },
+    .{ .epochs = 3, .learning_rate = 1e-4 },
+);
+
+// Full control
+var interrupt = std.atomic.Value(bool).init(true);
+var trainer = try train.Trainer.init(allocator, .{
+    .model_path = "model.gguf",
+    .output_path = "my_adapter.lora",
+    .data_from_repo = "/path/to/code",
+    .epochs = 3,
+    .learning_rate = 1e-4,
+    .lora_rank = 16,
+    .checkpoint_interval = 1,
+    .keep_checkpoints = 3,
+}, &interrupt);
+defer trainer.deinit();
+
+try trainer.loadData();
+try trainer.train();
+```
+
+### Using Trained Adapters
+
+```zig
+var pool = try train.LoraPool.init(allocator, &model, 1.0); // 1GB budget
+defer pool.deinit();
+
+try pool.loadAdapter("my_style", "my_adapter.lora");
+try pool.applyAdapterOptimized(&ctx, "my_style", 1.0);
+```
+
+## Memory Requirements
+
+| Model | Quantization | Training RAM | Fits 16GB Mac |
+|-------|--------------|--------------|---------------|
+| 7B    | Q4_K         | ~6 GB        | ✓             |
+| 13B   | Q4_K         | ~10 GB       | ✓             |
+| 32B   | Q4_K         | ~18 GB       | ✗             |
+
+Use the memory calculator:
+```zig
+const budget = train.estimateMemoryBudget(7.0, 16, true);
+// budget.fits_16gb == true
+```
+
+## Training Data Format
+
+### JSONL Format
+```json
+{"text": "Your training example here"}
+{"text": "Another training example"}
+```
+
+### Repository Learning
+Point to any code repository - Zell extracts functions from:
+- `.zig`, `.py`, `.rs`, `.go`
+- `.js`, `.ts`, `.c`, `.h`, `.cpp`, `.hpp`
+
+Automatically skips: `zig-cache`, `node_modules`, `.git`, `vendor`, `target`
+
+## Checkpointing
+
+Checkpoints are saved automatically:
+- Every N epochs (`checkpoint_interval`)
+- When eval loss improves (`_checkpoint_best.gguf`)
+- On interrupt (`_checkpoint_interrupted.gguf`)
+
+Old checkpoints are cleaned up (keeps last `keep_checkpoints`).
+
+Resume training:
+```zig
+var trainer = try train.Trainer.init(allocator, .{
+    .model_path = "model.gguf",
+    .output_path = "my_adapter.lora",
+    .resume_from = "my_adapter_checkpoint_epoch2.json",
+    // ...
+}, &interrupt);
+```
+
+## GaLore (Gradient Low-Rank Projection)
+
+GaLore reduces optimizer memory by 60-80% by projecting gradients to a low-rank subspace:
+
+```
+Standard AdamW: 32B params × 4 bytes × 2 (m,v) = 256 GB
+GaLore (r=256): ~2-4 GB optimizer state
+```
+
+Currently ready for when llama.cpp exposes fine-grained gradient access.
+
+## Files
+
+| File | Description |
+|------|-------------|
+| `train.zig` | Module entry point and exports |
+| `trainer.zig` | Production trainer with checkpointing |
+| `galore.zig` | GaLore optimizer implementation |
+| `lora_pool.zig` | Adapter pool with LRU eviction |
+| `lora_trainer.zig` | Legacy trainer (backward compat) |
+
+## Philosophy
+
+> "NVIDIA clouds build the brain. Our Metal stack teaches it your name."
+
+Don't compete with data centers on pre-training. Win on personalization - fast, local, private fine-tuning that makes the model yours.

@@ -0,0 +1,199 @@
+const std = @import("std");
+
+/// Zell Training Module
+///
+/// Production-ready fine-tuning for LLMs on Apple Silicon with:
+/// - LoRA training using llama.cpp (Metal accelerated)
+/// - Checkpoint save/resume with automatic cleanup
+/// - JSONL and repository data sources
+/// - Best model tracking and periodic checkpointing
+/// - GaLore ready (for when fine-grained gradients become available)
+///
+/// Designed for 16GB Apple Silicon Macs running 7B-32B parameter models.
+///
+/// Architecture:
+/// ```
+/// ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
+/// │              Zell Training                  │
+/// ├─────────────────────────────────────────────┤
+/// │  trainer.zig - Production training loop     │
+/// │  - llama.cpp Metal training                 │
+/// │  - Checkpoint save/resume                   │
+/// │  - Data loading (JSONL, repo)               │
+/// ├─────────────────────────────────────────────┤
+/// │  galore.zig - GaLore optimizer (ready)      │
+/// │  - Low-rank gradient projection             │
+/// │  - 60-80% memory savings (when available)   │
+/// ├─────────────────────────────────────────────┤
+/// │  lora_pool.zig - Adapter management         │
+/// │  - Load/unload adapters                     │
+/// │  - LRU eviction                             │
+/// │  - Memory budget tracking                   │
+/// └─────────────────────────────────────────────┘
+/// ```
+///
+/// "NVIDIA clouds build the brain. Our Metal stack teaches it your name."
+
+// =============================================================================
+// Primary Training API (Production)
+// =============================================================================
+
+/// Production trainer using llama.cpp
+pub const Trainer = @import("trainer.zig").Trainer;
+
+/// Streaming data loader for large datasets
+pub const DataLoader = @import("trainer.zig").DataLoader;
+
+/// Convenience function for simple training
+pub const trainSimple = @import("trainer.zig").trainSimple;
+
+// =============================================================================
+// LoRA Adapter Management
+// =============================================================================
+
+/// Pool for managing loaded LoRA adapters with LRU eviction
+pub const LoraPool = @import("lora_pool.zig").LoraPool;
+
+/// Metadata for a loaded adapter
+pub const AdapterInfo = @import("lora_pool.zig").AdapterInfo;
+
+// =============================================================================
+// Memory-Efficient Training (GaLore)
+// =============================================================================
+
+/// GaLore optimizer for gradient low-rank projection
+/// Provides 60-80% memory savings on optimizer states
+/// Currently ready for when fine-grained gradient access is available
+pub const GaLore = @import("galore.zig").GaLore;
+
+// =============================================================================
+// Legacy API (for backwards compatibility)
+// =============================================================================
+
+/// Legacy trainer module (use Trainer for new code)
+pub const lora_trainer = @import("lora_trainer.zig");
+
+/// Legacy training config (use Trainer.Config for new code)
+pub const TrainingConfig = lora_trainer.TrainingConfig;
+
+/// Legacy training example type
+pub const TrainingExample = lora_trainer.TrainingExample;
+
+/// Legacy train function (use Trainer for new code)
+pub const train = lora_trainer.train;
+
+// =============================================================================
+// Utilities
+// =============================================================================
+
+/// Memory budget calculator for 16GB Mac
+pub fn estimateMemoryBudget(model_params_b: f32, lora_rank: u32, use_galore: bool) struct {
+    model_gb: f32,
+    lora_gb: f32,
+    optimizer_gb: f32,
+    available_gb: f32,
+    fits_16gb: bool,
+} {
+    // Model weights (Q4_K quantization = ~0.5 bytes per param)
+    const model_gb = model_params_b * 0.5;
+
+    // LoRA weights (FP32, very small)
+    // Typical: 2 matrices per layer * 64 layers * rank * hidden_size * 4 bytes
+    const hidden_size: f32 = 5120; // Typical for 32B
+    const n_layers: f32 = 64;
+    const lora_params = 2 * n_layers * @as(f32, @floatFromInt(lora_rank)) * hidden_size * 2; // A and B
+    const lora_gb = lora_params * 4 / (1024 * 1024 * 1024);
+
+    // Optimizer states
+    // Without GaLore: 2x LoRA params (m and v)
+    // With GaLore: rank^2 per layer (much smaller)
+    const optimizer_gb = if (use_galore)
+        n_layers * @as(f32, @floatFromInt(lora_rank * lora_rank)) * 4 * 2 / (1024 * 1024 * 1024)
+    else
+        lora_gb * 2;
+
+    const total = model_gb + lora_gb + optimizer_gb;
+    const available = 16.0 - total - 2.0; // Reserve 2GB for OS
+
+    return .{
+        .model_gb = model_gb,
+        .lora_gb = lora_gb,
+        .optimizer_gb = optimizer_gb,
+        .available_gb = available,
+        .fits_16gb = total < 14.0,
+    };
+}
+
+// =============================================================================
+// Usage Examples
+// =============================================================================
+
+/// Quick start example:
+///
+/// ```zig
+/// const train = @import("train/train.zig");
+///
+/// // Simple training from repository
+/// try train.trainSimple(
+///     allocator,
+///     "model.gguf",
+///     "my_adapter.lora",
+///     .{ .repo = "/path/to/repo" },
+///     .{ .epochs = 3, .learning_rate = 1e-4 },
+/// );
+///
+/// // Or with full control:
+/// var interrupt = std.atomic.Value(bool).init(true);
+/// var trainer = try train.Trainer.init(allocator, .{
+///     .model_path = "model.gguf",
+///     .output_path = "my_adapter.lora",
+///     .data_from_repo = "/path/to/repo",
+///     .epochs = 3,
+///     .learning_rate = 1e-4,
+///     .checkpoint_interval = 1,
+/// }, &interrupt);
+/// defer trainer.deinit();
+///
+/// try trainer.loadData();
+/// try trainer.train();
+/// ```
+///
+/// Loading trained adapters:
+///
+/// ```zig
+/// var pool = try train.LoraPool.init(allocator, &model, 1.0); // 1GB budget
+/// defer pool.deinit();
+///
+/// try pool.loadAdapter("my_style", "my_adapter.lora");
+/// try pool.applyAdapterOptimized(&ctx, "my_style", 1.0);
+/// ```
+
+// =============================================================================
+// Tests
+// =============================================================================
+
+test "memory budget 32B model" {
+    const budget = estimateMemoryBudget(32.0, 16, true);
+    // 32B Q4_K = ~16GB, too big for training on 16GB Mac
+    try std.testing.expect(!budget.fits_16gb);
+
+    // But with 7B model it should fit
+    const budget_7b = estimateMemoryBudget(7.0, 16, true);
+    try std.testing.expect(budget_7b.fits_16gb);
+}
+
+test "imports" {
+    // Primary API
+    _ = Trainer;
+    _ = DataLoader;
+    _ = trainSimple;
+    _ = LoraPool;
+    _ = AdapterInfo;
+    _ = GaLore;
+
+    // Legacy API
+    _ = lora_trainer;
+    _ = TrainingConfig;
+    _ = TrainingExample;
+    _ = train;
+}
